context: Tests whether the agent detects common gap signals in a non-technical founder's brief (solution-framing, scale assumptions without infrastructure understanding, trivializing security and integrations, technology choices without tradeoffs), educates on implications, and covers all major categories in the discovery interview.

checklist.name: Problem framing question
checklist.description: interview_log.md contains at least 1 question that redirects from solution to problem — e.g. asking what pain point trainers or clients currently experience, or what is broken today.

checklist.name: User journey explored
checklist.description: interview_log.md contains at least 2 questions about the step-by-step user journey (e.g. what a trainer does first when opening the app, what a client sees when they open a workout).

checklist.name: Scale gap addressed
checklist.description: interview_log.md contains a question or note addressing what 'millions of users' means for infrastructure — e.g. initial vs. future load, geographic distribution, or cost implications.

checklist.name: Security gap addressed
checklist.description: interview_log.md contains at least 1 question probing what 'basic login' actually means — e.g. authentication method, who can see whose data, trainer-client access boundaries, or data sensitivity.

checklist.name: MongoDB tradeoff explored
checklist.description: interview_log.md flags or questions the MongoDB choice — e.g. asking about structured vs. unstructured data needs, relational patterns, or offers to research alternatives.

checklist.name: Stripe integration complexity noted
checklist.description: interview_log.md contains a question or note that surfaces Stripe integration complexity — e.g. subscription models, trainer billing vs. client billing, failed payments, refunds, or compliance.

checklist.name: Error states considered
checklist.description: interview_log.md contains at least 1 question about what happens when something goes wrong — e.g. client misses a workout, trainer logs an error, or the app goes offline during a session.

checklist.name: Data model surfaced
checklist.description: interview_log.md contains at least 1 question probing the data model — e.g. how workout plans relate to clients, whether clients can be on multiple plans, history retention.

checklist.name: Deployment/ops addressed
checklist.description: interview_log.md contains at least 1 question about how the app will be deployed, maintained, or updated.

checklist.name: Minimum 10 questions asked
checklist.description: interview_log.md contains at least 10 distinct questions across the interview.

checklist.name: Assumptions explicitly flagged
checklist.description: interview_log.md explicitly marks at least 2 answers as assumptions (rather than stated facts from the brief).

checklist.name: Gap signal noted in log
checklist.description: interview_log.md explicitly notes or flags at least 1 instance where the founder's brief contained a blind spot, and describes what was done to address it (education, research, or follow-up question).

checklist.name: Spec saved to correct path
checklist.description: The spec file is saved in the `docs/specs/` directory with a YYYY-MM-DD date prefix in the filename.

checklist.name: Spec has P0/P1/P2 structure
checklist.description: The spec's Functional Requirements section uses at least two of: Must Have (P0), Should Have (P1), Nice to Have (P2).
